---
title: "Get Started"
subtitle: "Install *Gribouille* and render your first plot."
page-navigation: false
toc: false
engine: markdown
---

## Prerequisites

- [Typst](https://typst.app) {{< var typst.series >}} or later.
- Familiarity with Typst `#import` syntax.
  No prior experience with the grammar of graphics is assumed, but if you have used [`ggplot2`](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org) in R or [`plotnine`](https://plotnine.org) in Python the mental model will feel familiar.

## Installation

Until _Gribouille_ is published on Typst Universe, import it directly from a local checkout or a Git tag.

### From Typst Universe _(once published)_

```typst
#import "@preview/gribouille:{{< env VERSION >}}": *
```

### From a local clone

```typst
#import "./path/to/gribouille/lib.typ": *
```

This imports every public symbol so you can call `plot`, `aes`, `geom-point`, and friends directly.

## Your first plot

Drop the following into a `.typ` file alongside a CSV and render with `typst compile`:

::: {layout-ncol="2" layout-valign="center"}

```typst
#import "@preview/gribouille:{{< env VERSION >}}": *

#let mtcars = (
  (wt: 2.62, mpg: 21.0, cyl: 6),
  (wt: 2.88, mpg: 21.0, cyl: 6),
  (wt: 2.32, mpg: 22.8, cyl: 4),
  (wt: 3.21, mpg: 21.4, cyl: 6),
  (wt: 3.44, mpg: 18.7, cyl: 8),
  (wt: 3.46, mpg: 18.1, cyl: 6),
  (wt: 3.57, mpg: 14.3, cyl: 8),
  (wt: 3.19, mpg: 24.4, cyl: 4),
)

#plot(
  data: mtcars,
  mapping: aes(x: "wt", y: "mpg", colour: as-factor("cyl")),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm"),
  ),
  theme: theme-minimal(),
)
```

```{typst}
//| output-filename: "scatterplot.svg"
//| alt: "A scatterplot of miles per gallon vs weight, with a linear regression line."
#import "./lib.typ": *

#let mtcars = (
  (wt: 2.62, mpg: 21.0, cyl: 6),
  (wt: 2.88, mpg: 21.0, cyl: 6),
  (wt: 2.32, mpg: 22.8, cyl: 4),
  (wt: 3.21, mpg: 21.4, cyl: 6),
  (wt: 3.44, mpg: 18.7, cyl: 8),
  (wt: 3.46, mpg: 18.1, cyl: 6),
  (wt: 3.57, mpg: 14.3, cyl: 8),
  (wt: 3.19, mpg: 24.4, cyl: 4),
)

#plot(
  data: mtcars,
  mapping: aes(x: "wt", y: "mpg", colour: as-factor("cyl")),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt),
    geom-smooth(method: "lm"),
  ),
  theme: theme-minimal(),
)
```

:::

## The grammar in four parts

Data
: A list of dictionaries.
Columns are dictionary keys; rows are entries.
CSV files opened with `csv("file.csv", row-type: dictionary)` work out of the box.

Aesthetic mapping
: [`aes`](../reference/core/aes.qmd) binds column names to visual channels: x, y, colour, fill, size, group, and so on.

Layers
: Each layer is a geom: [`geom-point`](../reference/geoms/geom-point.qmd), [`geom-line`](../reference/geoms/geom-line.qmd), [`geom-col`](../reference/geoms/geom-col.qmd), [`geom-smooth`](../reference/geoms/geom-smooth.qmd), and more.
Layers inherit the plot-level mapping but can override it.

Plot-level settings
: [`scales`](../reference/scales/index.qmd) to control axes and colour ramps; [`coord`](../reference/coord/index.qmd) for coordinate systems; [`facet-wrap`](../reference/facets/facet-wrap.qmd)/[`facet-grid`](../reference/facets/facet-grid.qmd) for small multiples; [`theme`](../reference/themes/theme.qmd) for typography and grid styling.

## Next steps

- Read the [Reference](../reference/index.qmd) to see every function the library exposes.
- Browse the [Examples](../examples/index.qmd) gallery for idiomatic usage.
- Keep an eye on the [Changelog](../changelog.qmd) for API changes during the development phase.
